scene1_hook: One million Americans wake up every day wondering which part of their body will betray them next.
scene2_problem: That's multiple sclerosis. It attacks the brain. It takes balance, sight, strength. It strikes women three times more than men, usually in their twenties.
scene3_stakes: A mother who can't pick up her toddler. A father who can't walk his daughter down the aisle. A young professional who sees the wheelchair coming, and can't stop it. Alone.
scene4_solution: Your nineteen dollars a month funds the exact research that's already slowing MS. New therapies mean more mornings without symptoms. More evenings still walking. More years before the wheelchair, if it ever comes at all.
scene5_ask: Become a Partner in Progress. Nineteen dollars a month. Visit nationalmssociety.org slash donate. Help someone keep the next morning.

lines: HELP SOMEONE KEEP THE NEXT MORNING
Douglas Morse has lived with MS since 1999
